steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student’s goal is to compare the Leech Lake Band of Ojibwe (1,311 sq mi in Minnesota) with the White Mountain Apache Tribe in Arizona. The blank asks for one specific value: the area of the White Mountain Apache Tribe in Arizona, read directly from the table.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘7,648 square miles.’: this is the area for a different tribal nation in the table, not the White Mountain Apache Tribe. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘2,631 square miles.’: this is the value shown in the table for the White Mountain Apache Tribe in Arizona — exactly the data point the student needs. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘4,419 square miles.’: this is the area for a different tribal nation in the table. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘3,606 square miles.’: this is the area for yet another tribal nation, not the White Mountain Apache Tribe. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
